 {"id":"gt-4nn.4","title":"Built-in molecules: engineer-in-box, quick-fix, research","description":"Create built-in molecules as Beads issues:

## engineer-in-box

```markdown
id: mol-engineer-in-box
type: molecule
title: Engineer in a Box

Full workflow from design to merge.

## Step: design
Think carefully about architecture. Consider:
- Existing patterns in the codebase
- Trade-offs between approaches  
- Testability and maintainability

Write a brief design summary before proceeding.

## Step: implement
Write the code. Follow codebase conventions.
Needs: design

## Step: review
Self-review the changes. Look for:
- Bugs and edge cases
- Style issues
- Missing error handling
Needs: implement

## Step: test
Write and run tests. Cover happy path and edge cases.
Fix any failures before proceeding.
Needs: implement

## Step: submit
Submit for merge via refinery.
Needs: review, test
```

## quick-fix

```markdown
id: mol-quick-fix
type: molecule  
title: Quick Fix

Fast path for small changes.

## Step: implement
Make the fix. Keep it focused.

## Step: test
Run relevant tests. Fix any regressions.
Needs: implement

## Step: submit
Submit for merge.
Needs: test
```

## research

```markdown
id: mol-research
type: molecule
title: Research

Investigation workflow.

## Step: investigate
Explore the question. Search code, read docs, 
understand context. Take notes.

## Step: document
Write up findings. Include:
- What you learned
- Recommendations
- Open questions
Needs: investigate
```

## Storage

Built-in molecules live in `<town>/.beads/` as regular issues.
Created during `gt install` or `bd init`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:04.574565-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:02:19.332406-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:02:19.332406-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.4","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:04.576587-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"gt-4u5z","title":"Refinery as Worktree: Local MR Integration","description":"Move refinery from separate clone to git worktree. Polecats stop pushing to origin - refinery sees their branches locally. MRs become wisps (ephemeral). Only main gets pushed after merge.

## Goals
- Origin stays clean (only main + beads-sync branches)
- No orphaned polecat branches ever
- Simpler mental model for MR coordination
- Faster integration (no network for local MR ops)

## Trade-offs
- Machine crash = redo pending work (acceptable, beads track state)
- Future federation needs different approach (bundles or integration branch)

## Components Affected
- Rig initialization (refinery setup)
- Spawn/polecat workflow (remove origin push)
- Refinery manager (local branch access)
- Documentation (architecture, workflows)
- Molecule templates (remove push instructions)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:24:56.517669-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:24:56.517669-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-4u5z.4","title":"Convert MR queue to wisp storage","description":"MR queue should use wisp storage (.beads-wisp/) instead of durable beads.

## Design (Simplified)

With refinery as worktree, polecats and refinery share the same machine. No cross-machine MR coordination needed. Wisps are the natural fit.

### Storage
- Location: `.beads-wisp/mq/<mr-id>.json`
- Format: Same MRFields structure (branch, target, source_issue, worker, rig)
- Lifecycle: Create on submit, delete after merge

### Code Changes

1. **MR Creation** (done.go, mq_submit.go)
   - Write to `.beads-wisp/mq/` instead of `bd create --type=merge-request`
   - Generate ID: `mr-<timestamp>-<random>` or similar

2. **Refinery Query** (refinery/manager.go, engineer.go)
   - Read from `.beads-wisp/mq/` directory
   - List files, parse JSON, process in order

3. **Cleanup**
   - Delete MR file after successful merge
   - On failure, leave file (refinery retries)

### What Stays in Beads
- Source issues (the work being merged) - still tracked in beads
- Merge commits reference source issue ID
- `bd close <issue>` still happens on merge

### Benefits
- No sync overhead for transient MR state
- Simpler model (file per MR, delete when done)
- Consistent with wisp philosophy (ephemeral operational state)

### Not Needed
- Digest squashing (MRs are deleted, not accumulated)
- Cross-machine visibility (single-machine architecture)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:29.716788-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:22:32.180049-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T21:22:32.180049-08:00","close_reason":"MR queue now uses wisp storage (.beads-wisp/mq/). MRs are created as JSON files, read by refinery, deleted after merge.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.4","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:29.717201-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.4","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:43.719801-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-gby","title":"gt handoff: Unified agent lifecycle command","description":"## Summary

Unified `gt handoff` command for ALL agent types to request lifecycle actions.

## Usage

gt handoff              # Context-aware default
gt handoff --shutdown   # Terminate, cleanup, don't restart
gt handoff --cycle      # Restart with handoff mail
gt handoff --restart    # Fresh restart, no handoff

## Context-Aware Defaults

| Agent Type | Default | Reason |
|------------|---------|--------|
| Polecat | --shutdown | Ephemeral, work is done |
| Witness | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Refinery | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Mayor | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Crew | (sends mail only) | Human-managed |

## What gt handoff Does

1. **Verify safe to stop**
   - Git state clean (no uncommitted changes)
   - Work handed off (PR exists for polecats)

2. **Send handoff mail to self** (for cycle/restart)
   - Captures current state
   - New session will read this

3. **Send lifecycle request to manager**
   - Polecats/Refinery → Witness
   - Witness/Mayor → Daemon
   - Format: mail to <manager> with action type

4. **Set state: requesting_<action>**
   - Lifecycle manager checks this before acting

5. **Wait for termination**
   - Don't self-exit - let manager kill session
   - Ensures clean handoff

## Lifecycle Request Flow

Agent                           Lifecycle Manager
  |                                    |
  | 1. gt handoff --cycle              |
  |    a. Verify git clean             |
  |    b. Send handoff mail to self    |
  |    c. Set requesting_cycle=true    |
  |    d. Send lifecycle request       |
  |------------------------------------→|
  |                                    |
  |                   2. Receive request
  |                   3. Verify state  |
  |                   4. Kill session  |
  |                   5. Start new     |
  |                      (for cycle)   |
  |                                    |
  |         New session reads handoff  |
  |         Resumes work               |

## Who Manages Whom

| Agent | Sends lifecycle request to |
|-------|---------------------------|
| Polecat | <rig>/witness |
| Refinery | <rig>/witness |
| Witness | daemon/ |
| Mayor | daemon/ |

## Implementation

1. Detect current role (polecat, witness, refinery, mayor, crew)
2. Apply context-aware default if no flag specified
3. Run pre-flight checks (git clean, work handed off)
4. Send handoff mail to self (if cycling)
5. Send lifecycle request to appropriate manager
6. Set requesting_<action> in state.json
7. Wait (manager will kill us)

## For Polecats (--shutdown)

Additional cleanup after kill:
- Witness removes worktree
- Witness deletes polecat branch
- Polecat ceases to exist

## Related Issues

- gt-99m: Daemon (handles Mayor/Witness lifecycle)
- gt-7ik: Ephemeral polecats (polecat cleanup)
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:40.806863-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:18:22.35369-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.423945-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-eu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.547204-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:50:24.142182-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-gl2","title":"Clarify Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities","description":"Document the cleanup authority model: Witness owns ALL per-worker cleanup, Mayor never involved.

## The Rule

**Witness handles ALL per-worker cleanup. Mayor is never involved.**

## Why This Matters

1. Separation of concerns: Mayor strategic, Witness operational
2. Reduced coordination overhead: No back-and-forth for routine cleanup
3. Faster shutdown: Witness kills workers immediately upon verification
4. Cleaner escalation: Mayor only hears about problems

## What Witness Handles

- Verifying worker git state before kill
- Nudging workers to fix dirty state
- Killing worker sessions
- Updating worker state (sleep/wake)
- Logging verification results

## What Mayor Handles

- Receiving swarm complete notifications
- Deciding whether to start new swarms
- Handling escalations (stuck workers after 3 retries)
- Cross-rig coordination

## Escalation Path

Worker stuck -> Witness nudges (up to 3x) -> Witness escalates to Mayor -> Mayor decides: force kill, reassign, or human

## Anti-Patterns

DO NOT: Mayor asks Witness if worker X is clean
DO: Witness reports swarm complete, all workers verified

DO NOT: Mayor kills worker sessions directly
DO: Mayor tells Witness to abort swarm, Witness handles cleanup

DO NOT: Workers report done to Mayor
DO: Workers report to Witness, Witness aggregates and reports up","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:48:56.678724-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:12.068964-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gl2","depends_on_id":"gt-82y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:49:05.929877-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-gl6s","title":"gt spawn: polecats report success but no work actually happens","description":"## Problem

gt spawn appears to succeed (creates worktree, sends mail notifications, reports 'Session started') but:
1. No actual Claude session runs
2. Polecats send POLECAT_DONE messages without doing work
3. No code is committed or pushed
4. MR issues are created but branches don't exist

## Observed Behavior

Spawned 12 polecats with gt spawn beads/<name> --create --issue <id>
- All reported 'Session started'
- All sent POLECAT_STARTED notifications
- All sent POLECAT_DONE notifications
- But: no worktrees remained, no branches pushed, no code written

## Expected Behavior

Polecats should actually run Claude Code and complete assigned work before sending POLECAT_DONE.

## Impact

Silent failure causes false completion signals, wasted witness time processing fake completions, and stale MR/assignee pollution in beads.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:22:45.498303-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:22:45.498303-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-gl6s","title":"gt spawn: polecats report success but no work actually happens","description":"## Problem

gt spawn polecats have multiple silent failure modes:

### Failure Mode 1: No gt prime
- Claude starts at prompt and waits for input
- SessionStart hook doesn't auto-run 'gt prime'
- Without gt prime, polecats don't pick up work

### Failure Mode 2: No git push (CRITICAL)
- Polecat commits code locally
- Polecat closes the beads issue
- Polecat sends POLECAT_DONE
- **But code is never pushed to remote branch**
- When worktree is cleaned up, commits are lost forever
- Issue appears closed but no code exists

## Evidence

testcat on bd-d28c:
- Reported 'Work Complete' with commits 7d3447b9, cd87e541
- Closed bd-d28c
- Submitted MR bd-yx22
- But: `grep -r 'TestCreateTombstone' cmd/bd/*.go` returns nothing
- No polecat/testcat branch on remote

## Required Fixes

1. Polecats MUST push their branch before closing issues
2. Witness MUST verify branch exists on remote before cleanup
3. gt spawn should auto-nudge 'gt prime'
4. Consider: Polecat handoff should fail if unpushed commits exist

## Verification Protocol for Witness

Before accepting POLECAT_DONE:
```bash
# 1. Verify branch pushed
git branch -r | grep polecat/<name>

# 2. Verify code exists
grep -r '<expected_function>' path/to/files

# 3. Only then cleanup
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:22:45.498303-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:36:55.53829-08:00"}

Usage from mol-witness-patrol survey-workers step:
```bash
bd mol bond mol-polecat-arm $PATROL_WISP_ID \
  --ref arm-$polecat \
  --var polecat_name=$polecat \
  --var rig=<rig>
```

This creates child wisps like patrol-x7k.arm-ace with variables expanded.

Implementation:
1. Add 'bond' subcommand to mol command
2. Accept: proto ID, parent ID, --ref for child suffix, --var key=value pairs
3. Call InstantiateMolecule with Context map populated from --var flags
4. Return created child ID

 {"id":"gt-iu23","title":"Polecat doesn't auto-start after spawn inject - requires manual nudge","description":"## Problem

After `gt spawn --issue <id> --create`, the polecat session shows Claude Code started with the injected prompt, but Claude doesn't begin processing. The prompt just sits there until manually nudged.

## Evidence

```
$ gt spawn --issue gt-rixa --rig gastown --create
...
✓ Session started. Attach with: gt session at gastown/furiosa
  Polecat nudged to start working
  Witness notified to monitor startup
```

Session shows:
```
> You have a work assignment. Run 'gt mail inbox' to see it, then start
  working on issue gt-rixa.

  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)
```

But Claude doesn't respond. Manual nudge required:
```
$ gt nudge gt-gastown-furiosa "Please start working..."
```

After nudge, polecat immediately starts working correctly.

## Hypothesis

The spawn inject happens before Claude Code is fully initialized. The text arrives in the input buffer, but Claude hasn't started listening yet. By the time Claude starts, the input has already been 'consumed' as initial prompt text but not submitted.

## Resolution Plan

This will be solved by the **polecat molecule workflow** (mol-polecat-work), which provides structured lifecycle management. The molecule approach handles startup, work, and shutdown as discrete steps with proper state tracking.

**Blocked on**: beads/crew/dave completing ephemeral molecules (bd mol bond, ephemeral beads repo).

## Workaround

For now, use `gt nudge` if a polecat doesn't start within ~30 seconds of spawn.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:06:27.375686-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:53:46.940942-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-odvf","title":"Document bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI","description":"Create CLI reference documentation for molecule commands:

## bd mol bond

Instantiate a proto into a runnable molecule.

```bash
bd mol bond <proto-id> [--wisp] [--assignee=<addr>]
```

- Default: creates a Mol (durable, in main beads)
- --wisp: creates a Wisp (ephemeral, in .beads-ephemeral/)
- --assignee: who will execute this molecule

## bd mol squash

Complete a molecule and generate digest.

```bash
bd mol squash <mol-id> --summary='...'
```

- For Mol: creates digest in git history
- For Wisp: evaporates (no permanent record)
- --summary: required summary of what was accomplished

## bd mol burn

Abandon a molecule without completing.

```bash
bd mol burn <mol-id> [--reason='...']
```

- Discards molecule state
- No digest created
- Use when molecule is no longer needed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:06.462105-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T02:14:23.810794-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T02:14:23.810794-08:00","close_reason":"Already documented in docs/molecules.md lines 70-235","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-odvf","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.530156-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-ogpk","title":"Add neighbor-check steps to all patrol molecules","description":"Part of the 'Gas Town is a Village' antifragility design.

Every patrol molecule should include optional neighbor-checking:
- Deacon checks Witness and Refinery health
- Witness checks Refinery health  
- Refinery checks Witness health
- Polecats can peek other polecats

Use gt peek to check health states.
If stuck neighbor found, can nudge or escalate.

This creates distributed monitoring - no single point of failure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:01:16.358314-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:01:16.358314-08:00"}
